failure_surface: Formula applied to multiple rows contains identical cell references (e.g., B2, C2) across all cells instead of incrementing row numbers per row (e.g., B2/B3/B4...)
root_cause: Agent assigned the same literal formula string to every target cell without adjusting relative cell references for each row position, causing all cells to reference row 2 data regardless of their actual row
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When applying formulas to multiple rows via code, dynamically construct each formula string with row-specific references or use openpyxl features that preserve relative reference semantics during multi-cell application
evidence: Verification output shows Row 3 has value '=IF(B2=2022,C2,INDEX($C2:$N2,MATCH($A2,$C$1:$N$1,0)))' instead of '=IF(B3=2022,C3,INDEX($C3:$N3,MATCH($A3,$C$1:$N$1,0)))'
All rows 2-13 contain identical formula string with hardcoded row 2 references
Loop iterates through rows 2-14 but does not modify formula string inside loop body to reflect current row number

memory_items.title: Relative Reference Handling in Multi-Row Formula Application
memory_items.lesson: When distributing a formula pattern across multiple rows programmatically, each cell must receive row-adjusted references; a single static string will not auto-update across rows
memory_items.generalization: Use dynamic string formatting or row-index variables when constructing formulas for each target cell, especially for relative references like A2 vs A3

memory_items.title: Formula Verification After Bulk Assignment
memory_items.lesson: After assigning formulas to a range, verify at least 2-3 different rows show correct incremental references, not just the first cell
memory_items.generalization: Sampling multiple positions in the affected range catches row-reference errors before saving

memory_items.title: Understanding openpyxl Formula Syntax Semantics
memory_items.lesson: Setting cell.value to a formula string treats it as literal text; openpyxl does not automatically interpret or convert relative references when copying strings between cells
memory_items.generalization: When manipulating formulas via openpyxl, either build formulas programmatically per-cell or use worksheet.cell().formula with proper row context

edits.content:
### 6. Multi-Row Formula Application
- **Dynamic References:** When applying formulas to multiple cells, construct each formula with the correct row index; static strings will duplicate identical references across all cells.
- **Verification Strategy:** Sample formulas from non-contiguous rows in the target range to confirm relative references incremented properly.
- **Code Pattern:** Use loop variables to insert current row number into formula strings (e.g., f'=SUM(A{row}:B{row})') rather than copying fixed text.
